visits.note: Patient P043 is a 55-year-old man presenting with new onset of fatigue and polyuria. Physical examination reveals obesity (BMI 32). Initial lab results show HbA1c of 7.2, BP 140/90, eGFR 72, and LDL 130. The patient is diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. Medications include Metformin 500mg twice daily, Lisinopril 10mg daily, and Atorvastatin 20mg daily.

Patient P043 returns with worsening fatigue and shortness of breath. Physical examination reveals no new findings. Lab results show HbA1c of 8.1, BP 150/95, eGFR 68, and LDL 145. The patient's medications are adjusted to Metformin 850mg twice daily, Lisinopril 20mg daily, and Atorvastatin 40mg daily. A diagnosis of Coronary Artery Disease is added due to new symptoms.

Patient P043 returns with worsening kidney function and new onset of edema. Physical examination reveals pitting edema bilaterally. Lab results show HbA1c of 7.5, BP 148/98, eGFR 58, and LDL 155. The patient's medications are adjusted to Metformin 1000mg twice daily, Lisinopril 30mg daily, and Atorvastatin 80mg daily. A diagnosis of Chronic Kidney Disease is added due to worsening eGFR.

Patient P043 returns with stable lab values and improved symptoms. Physical examination reveals no new findings. Lab results show HbA1c of 7.0, BP 140/80, eGFR 65, and LDL 120. The patient's medications remain unchanged.
